LAW AND MOTION, 302, RULING: PLAINTIFF DID NOT GIVE NOTICE TO OPPOSING COUNSEL THAT HE WILL BE APPEARING TO CONTEST THE TENTATIVE RULING. THE COURT DID NOT HEAR ARGUMENT AND ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. PLAINTIFF RUDOLPH FISHER'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DISMISSAL ORDER IS OFF CALENDAR. THE MOVING PARTY FAILED TO PROVIDE A PROOF OF SERVICE AND THE MOTION IS UNTIMELY UNDER CCP 1005. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR# 5976.

calendar.matters:
Mtn To Set Aside Dismissal Order
Off Calendar Mar-12-2014 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Rudolph Fisher, Tel. 415.571.3922, plaintiff and moving party appearing on his own behalf;
No other appearance.

RULING: Plaintiff did not give notice to opposing counsel that he will be appearing to contest the tentative ruling. The Court did not hear argument and adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF RUDOLPH FISHER's Motion To Set Aside Dismissal Order is off calendar. The moving party failed to provide a proof of service and the motion is untimely under CCP 1005. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR# 5976.

Off Calendar Feb-19-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: RUDOLPH FISHER, 415.337.8927, PLAINTIFF IN PRO PER.
BRADLEY RUSSI, DEPUTY CITY ATTORNEY, 415.554.3800, FOR DEFENDANTS LEUTENANT STEPHEN TILTON, DEPUTY EVAN STAEHLEY AND DEPUTY SCOTT NEU.

Judge Busch DISCLOSES that his wife, Catharine Barnes, is a deputy city attorney. She is assigned to the contracts team, she does not normally handle litigation, and she has had no involvement of any kind in this litigation. Judge Busch does not consider her employment a basis for his recusal.

RULING: Argued and the Court GRANTED DEFENDANTS LEUTENANT STEPHEN TILTON, DEPUTY EVAN STAEHLEY AND DEPUTY SCOTT NEU'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS AND COMPLAINT. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Anthony Vaughn #6185.
